wf_hld_desc: Design the overall architecture and relations between components.
wf_lld_desc: Detail the physical design specs, hardware, and software technologies.
wf_req_locked: Requirements are completed and cannot be modified.
eod_desc: Plan and prioritize your top 6 goals for tomorrow at the end of the day.
eod_empty: You haven't planned any goals for tomorrow yet.
eod_max_limit: According to the Ivy Lee method, you should focus on a maximum of 6 goals per day.
notepad_desc: Safely store your ideas, drafts, and notes.
notepad_no_notes: No notes created yet. Add a new note from the top left.
flowchart_desc: Map out process steps and decision points.
welcome_msg: Log in to save your projects to the cloud.
hero_subtitle: Map out complex workflows, find root causes, and maximize productivity with Solvinger.
ws_subtitle: Combine project management and problem-solving methodologies in one platform. Select a tool to start.
wbs_desc: Break your project down into smaller, manageable parts.
whys_desc: Ask 'Why?' consecutively to get to the root cause of the problem.
swot_desc: Evaluate your project's strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats.
ishi_desc: Systematically analyze root causes based on 6M categories.
pdca_desc: Ensure continuous improvement with Plan, Do, Check, Act phases.
wf_desc: Manage your project sequentially with Requirements, Design, Implementation, Verification, and Maintenance phases.

pdca_subtitle: Follow Plan, Do, Check, Act steps for continuous improvement.

fta_desc: Analyze root causes of failures using AND/OR logic gates
wf_subtitle: Apply the traditional phase-based project management model.

do_desc: Test the plan on a small scale
req_desc: Define what the system should do.
des_desc: Plan the architecture and technical details.
ver_desc: Verify that the system meets requirements.
